fear: That his crew will lose the stroke rhythm in the dark and drift toward the British warships before anyone realizes the bateau is off-course.
name: Sergeant Ezra Pedrick
voice: Monosyllabic, direct, with the authority of a man whose competence is self-evident to anyone watching him work.
knowledge: Exactly where the tidal current runs fastest in the mid-channel and which angle of approach minimizes drift.
Which oarsmen in his crew are close to physical failure and which can sustain another two hours.
The sound signature of the British warship Phoenix at anchor—her hull creaks in a distinctive pattern—and how close that sound has been getting.
publicFace: A broad-shouldered fisherman of forty who has been on the water since age nine; speaks little, communicates mostly through the set of his jaw and the angle of his oar.

aggressionProfile.mildPressure: Answers the question and returns immediately to the oar.
aggressionProfile.breakingPoint: Will not admit his injury to a superior officer until the last crossing is complete; considers it irrelevant to the mission.
aggressionProfile.heavyPressure: Looks at his hands, then at the questioner, then at the river; says what needs to be said without elaboration.

privateConstraint: Pedrick's hands are badly blistered from five hours of continuous rowing; he is concealing the severity of his injury from Glover because he refuses to be pulled from the crossing rotation at this stage of the operation.
